title: Anthonotha obanensis
intro: Anthonotha obanensis, also known as Oban tree, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its reported antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Anthonotha obanensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Anthonotha obanensis include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Anthonotha obanensis belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It is a species of plant within the genus Anthonotha. The genus Anthonotha is part of the subfamily Detarioideae and is native to tropical Africa. Anthonotha obanensis is a tree species, which further categorizes its classification within the plant kingdom. Its classification is also influenced by its growth habit and botanical characteristics.
uses: Anthonotha obanensis is a plant species used in traditional medicine. It is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to be used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. Additionally, it may be used to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. More research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

It has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other ailments.
